html:
The Vendetta Gazette

Vol. XII • May 18, 2026 • “All the Blood That’s Fit to Print” • Two Cents (If You’re Still Alive to Pay)

★ TERRITORY GAINS THIS WEEK ★ Donald Trump     +41
Cokro            +20
Blood Captain    +3
Wu-Tang          +2
Pawluo82         +1

The Emperor Tightens His Golden Fist — Forty-One More Blocks Fall to Trump!

Cokro's War Machine Roars to Life; Blood Captain Plays Both Sides of the Ledger By Our Man in the Trenches, Ignatius P. Flambeau

Merciful heavens above and all the devils below, have we not suffered enough of this man's ambition? Donald Trump, the Platinum Pompadour himself, the Golden Emperor whose gilded shadow falls across half of Vendetta City like a funeral shroud, has gone and done it again. Forty-one — count them, gentle reader, forty-one — additional turfs absorbed into his ever-swelling dominion between the fourth and eleventh of May. Last week your correspondent marvelled at thirty-seven. This week the man has somehow found another gear entirely.

And yet — and here is the morsel that shall keep the gossips busy from the waterfront to the uptown parlours — the Emperor's army shrank. Sources within the Trump organisation confirm that a staggering three hundred and twenty-seven garrison units have vanished from the rolls this week alone, leaving the great man with a still-formidable five thousand and twenty-two souls under arms. Desertions? Battlefield attrition? Strategic repositioning of a general playing a longer game than any of us can fathom? The Emperor's press attaché, a laconic bruiser known only as "Marble-Mouth" McGee, refused all comment. What we do know is that Trump's expansion was achieved through surgical territorial assaults — capturing ground methodically, block by block — while five raids netted his operation a tidy supplementary income from neighbours too slow or too careless to keep their vaults locked.

Cokro's Cannonade — Twenty Turfs and an Army That Grows By the Hour

By Our Man in the Trenches, Ignatius P. Flambeau

If last week's edition introduced Cokro — "the Eastside Anvil," as the dockworkers have taken to calling him — as a man of rising promise, this week demands we treat him as a genuine contender for the throne. Twenty turfs added to his portfolio in a single week, and that is not the half of it. Cokro's recruiters have been working the back alleys and the gin joints without pause: twenty-eight fresh garrison units now swell his ranks, bringing his total to a muscular twelve hundred strong. The man is building, and building fast. Whether he is constructing a fortress or a battering ram aimed squarely at the Emperor's front door remains the question on every underworld lip in Vendetta City.

Meanwhile, Brimex — who last week quietly claimed a solitary turf and drew barely a paragraph's notice — has apparently spent the intervening days in a fury of recruitment. Thirty-three new soldiers added to his garrison, now three hundred and forty in total. No territorial advances to speak of, but a man who arms himself so rapidly without yet striking is either very patient or very frightened. This correspondent suspects the former.

Blood Captain — Buccaneer of the Boardrooms, Plunderer of the Unwary

By Our Man in the Trenches, Ignatius P. Flambeau

There is no more complicated figure in this week's criminal almanac than Blood Captain, the seafaring scoundrel of Vendetta City's underworld, who this week operated with the gleeful opportunism of a man who has read the rulebook twice and circled all the loopholes. Three territorial captures extended his domain, but it is his raiding record that truly dazzles: eight raids executed with professional precision, terrorising the establishments of Jason Bourne, Blood Captain's own rivals, and the hapless Wcrypto, among others.

And then — oh, the audacity of it — Blood Captain had the brass neck to reach directly into Donald Trump's own vaults. The single most consequential raid of the entire week saw
